risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Consenting patients were randomly assigned to the GIN group or the control group on the day of the surgery with the help of an assistant and by using a centralized computer system assigning patients to groups in all participating facilities." Comment: This describes a centralized computer system used for random assignment, which is a proper randomization method and provides sufficient context to judge the allocation sequence as truly random.

_evidence.method_quote: Consenting patients were randomly assigned to the GIN group or the control group on the day of the surgery with the help of an assistant and by using a centralized computer system assigning patients to groups in all participating facilities.
_evidence.method_summary: Patients were randomly assigned using a centralized computer system on the day of surgery.
context_clues: assigning patients to groups in all participating facilities

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Consenting patients were randomly assigned to the GIN group or the control group on the day of the surgery with the help of an assistant and by using a centralized computer system assigning patients to groups in all participating facilities." Comment: This explicitly describes centralized computer allocation, which prevents the enrolling clinician from foreseeing the next assignment; therefore allocation concealment is adequately reported and at low risk.

_evidence.concealment_quote: Consenting patients were randomly assigned to the GIN group or the control group on the day of the surgery with the help of an assistant and by using a centralized computer system assigning patients to groups in all participating facilities.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Patients in this study participated in a larger registered Randomized Controlled Trial on the effect of intraoperative GIN therapy on outcomes after cardiac surgery" and "All surgeries took place in the morning hours of the day... In the GIN group a priming bolus of 2 U insulin was followed by an infusion... In the control group, arterial blood glucose measurements were performed every 30 to 60 minutes..." Comment: The study describes different intraoperative treatment procedures for GIN versus control and does not report any blinding of participants or personnel; the extracted evidence explicitly states participants blinded = no, personnel blinded = no, blinding method = none described. Because the outcomes were primarily subjective/neuropsychological tests and self-report questionnaires, knowledge of allocation could plausibly influence performance on these outcomes, so this domain is High risk.

outcome_categories: Questionnaires (self-esteem, depression, chronic stress, social support)
subjective_outcomes: Questionnaires (self-esteem, depression, chronic stress, social support)

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Cognitive function was assessed during three home visits" and "Questionnaires measuring specific traits known to affect cognitive performance, such as self-esteem, depression, chronic stress and social support were also administered." Comment: The outcome assessment was performed during home visits and included multiple subjective, judgement-dependent outcomes (RAVLT, digit span, trail making, word pair association) plus patient-reported questionnaires. The article does not state that the outcome assessor was blinded, and these outcomes could be influenced by knowledge of allocation; under the worst-outcome rule, the unblinded/uncertain assessment of subjective outcomes makes the domain high risk even though some outcomes (e.g. blood glucose, infections) are objective.

outcomes.label: Self-esteem, depression, chronic stress, social support questionnaires

risk_of_bias.support_text: Summary: The article reports only that "Of the 50 patients approached 26 completed the study: 14 patients in the GIN and 12 in the control group" and the extracted evidence notes that per-arm randomized totals and reasons for loss are not described, with missing data handling "not described." Comment: Although total completion was low, the paper does not provide enough post-randomization attrition detail on the analysed outcome to determine whether loss was differential or outcome-related; per the domain rules, absence of CONSORT-style numbers is Unclear rather than High.

per_arm_flow.reasons: "Of the 50 patients approached 26 completed the study: 14 patients in the GIN ... group"
"Of the 50 patients approached 26 completed the study: ... 12 in the control group"

_evidence.notes: Attrition is reported only as total completion numbers from the 50 approached to the 26 who completed the study; no arm-level randomised totals or reasons for loss are described.
